name: Gae Buide
description: Gae Buide, meaning "Yellow Spear" in Irish, is a legendary spear from Irish mythology, often associated with Diarmuid Ua Duibhne (Diarmuid O'Dyna). It is one of two spears possessed by Diarmuid, the other being Gae Dearg (Red Spear). Gae Buide is renowned for its piercing accuracy and is often described as being unerring in its aim, never missing its target. Its power and significance stem not only from its physical properties but also from its connection to Diarmuid, a hero famed for his beauty, bravery, and tragic love affair with Gráinne. The spear represents Diarmuid's skill as a warrior and his mastery of weaponry. While Gae Dearg is often emphasized for its use in direct combat, Gae Buide is seen as more of a precision weapon, used for hunting or specific strikes requiring pinpoint accuracy. The legacy of Gae Buide continues to fascinate, symbolizing the prowess of Irish warriors and the power imbued in legendary weapons.
subtitle: Diarmuid's unerring yellow spear.
origin: Forged by a skilled smith for Diarmuid Ua Duibhne.
currentLocation: Lost, possibly buried with Diarmuid or hidden after his death.

keyMyths.title: The Pursuit of Diarmuid and Gráinne
keyMyths.description: During their flight, Diarmuid uses Gae Buide to hunt for food, showcasing its unerring accuracy in bringing down game. It highlights his resourcefulness and skill, ensuring their survival while being pursued by Fionn mac Cumhaill. The spear's success in these hunts is crucial to their survival.
keyMyths.source: Various versions of the Diarmuid and Gráinne tale

keyMyths.title: Diarmuid's Final Hunt
keyMyths.description: In some versions of the story, Diarmuid is unarmed when hunting the boar that ultimately kills him. In others, he chooses not to use Gae Buide, knowing his fate. This decision underscores the themes of destiny and the limitations of even the most powerful weapons in the face of predetermined events. His refusal to use it highlights the fated end.

keyMyths.title: The Two Spears of Diarmuid
keyMyths.description: The contrast between Gae Buide and Gae Dearg reflects different aspects of Diarmuid's character and fighting style. Gae Dearg represents brute force and direct combat, while Gae Buide symbolizes precision and skill. This duality underscores Diarmuid's versatility as a warrior and his ability to adapt to different situations.
keyMyths.source: Analysis of Diarmuid's weapons in Celtic literature

sources.author: Cross, Tom Peete
sources.description: Provides a comprehensive index of motifs found in early Irish literature, including those associated with legendary weapons like Gae Buide.
